title: Users may experience issues accessing or using Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365)
impact: Affected users may have been unable to use Copilot services and features.

updates.content:
Title: Users may experience issues accessing or using Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365)

User impact: Affected users may be unable to use Copilot services and features.

Current status: We've received reports indicating that some users are unable to access Copilot or use Copilot services and features. We're investigating the reports and reviewing our telemetry to identify the source of the issue.

Scope of impact: We're reviewing telemetry to identify the full scope of impact. This field will be updated as soon as additional information is available.

We’re reviewing service telemetry and examining recent update history as we work to identify the full scope and underlying cause of impact.

This quick update is designed to give the latest information on this issue.

Title: Users may experience issues accessing or using Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365)

User impact: Affected users may be unable to use Copilot services and features.

More info: Prompts to Copilot may return an error message stating "Oops, Something happened. Can you try again?"

Current status: We're reviewing specific segments of our service infrastructure that are generating errors as we work to isolate the cause of impact and develop an appropriate remediation strategy.

Scope of impact: Our telemetry indicates that impact is primarily to users hosted in the North America; however, users in other regions may also be impacted.

Start time: Friday, April 17, 2026, at 2:10 PM UTC

Next update by: Friday, April 17, 2026, at 4:30 PM UTC

Title: Users may experience issues accessing or using Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365)

User impact: Affected users may be unable to use Copilot services and features.

More info: This issue primarily affects the Microsoft 365 Copilot app. Some users have reported that they are able to use the web version of Copilot without issue.

Prompts to Copilot may return an error message stating "Oops, Something happened. Can you try again?"

Copilot services and features within Microsoft Teams are also affected by this issue.

Current status: We've identified the potential source of impact through our review of service telemetry and are working to implement additional processing throughput across the affected region as part of our mitigation strategy.

Scope of impact: Our telemetry indicates that impact is primarily to users hosted in the North America; however, users in other regions may also be impacted.

Start time: Friday, April 17, 2026, at 2:10 PM UTC

Next update by: Friday, April 17, 2026, at 5:00 PM UTC

Title: Users may experience issues accessing or using Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365)

User impact: Affected users may be unable to use Copilot services and features.

More info: This issue primarily affected the Microsoft 365 Copilot app. Some users reported that they were able to use the web version of Copilot without issue during the event. 

Prompts to Copilot returned an error message stating "Oops, Something happened. Can you try again?" 

Copilot services and features within Microsoft Teams were also affected by this issue.

Final status: We've increased processing throughput across the affected service infrastructure and confirmed through our telemetry that the impact has been remediated. The issue is now resolved. We will continue to closely monitor the service and apply additional mitigations if necessary.

Scope of impact: Our telemetry indicates that impact was primarily to users hosted in North America; however, users in other regions may have also been impacted.

Start time: Friday, April 17, 2026, at 2:00 PM UTC

End Time: Friday, April 17, 2026, at 4:30 PM UTC 

Preliminary root cause: A portion of our service infrastructure was temporarily unable to process user requests made to Copilot, resulting in impact.

Next steps:
     - We’re reviewing processing throughput metrics to ensure that we’re able to handle any future influx in service load.

We’ll provide a preliminary post-incident report in two business days, and a full post-incident report in five business days.
A post-incident report has been published.
